item.title: MediX-R1: Open Ended Medical Reinforcement Learning
item.description: We introduce MediX-R1, an open-ended Reinforcement Learning (RL) framework for medical multimodal large language models (MLLMs) that enables clinically grounded, free-form answers beyond multiple-choice formats. MediX-R1 fine-tunes a bas...

item.title: MobilityBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Route-Planning Agents in Real-World Mobility Scenarios
item.description: Route-planning agents powered by large language models (LLMs) have emerged as a promising paradigm for supporting everyday human mobility through natural language interaction and tool-mediated decision making. However, systematic evaluat...

item.title: From Blind Spots to Gains: Diagnostic-Driven Iterative Training for Large Multimodal Models
item.description: As Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) scale up and reinforcement learning (RL) methods mature, LMMs have made notable progress in complex reasoning and decision making. Yet training still relies on static data and fixed recipes, making it di...

item.title: OmniGAIA: Towards Native Omni-Modal AI Agents
item.description: Human intelligence naturally intertwines omni-modal perception -- spanning vision, audio, and language -- with complex reasoning and tool usage to interact with the world. However, current multi-modal LLMs are primarily confined to bi-mo...

item.title: Exploratory Memory-Augmented LLM Agent via Hybrid On- and Off-Policy Optimization
item.description: Exploration remains the key bottleneck for large language model agents trained with reinforcement learning. While prior methods exploit pretrained knowledge, they fail in environments requiring the discovery of novel states. We propose E...

item.title: AI Gamestore: Scalable, Open-Ended Evaluation of Machine General Intelligence with Human Games
item.description: Rigorously evaluating machine intelligence against the broad spectrum of human general intelligence has become increasingly important and challenging in this era of rapid technological advance. Conventional AI benchmarks typically assess...

item.title: Risk-Aware World Model Predictive Control for Generalizable End-to-End Autonomous Driving
item.description: With advances in imitation learning (IL) and large-scale driving datasets, end-to-end autonomous driving (E2E-AD) has made great progress recently. Currently, IL-based methods have become a mainstream paradigm: models rely on standard dr...

item.title: Echoes Over Time: Unlocking Length Generalization in Video-to-Audio Generation Models
item.description: Scaling multimodal alignment between video and audio is challenging, particularly due to limited data and the mismatch between text descriptions and frame-level video information. In this work, we tackle the scaling challenge in multimod...

item.title: Imagination Helps Visual Reasoning, But Not Yet in Latent Space
item.description: Latent visual reasoning aims to mimic human's imagination process by meditating through hidden states of Multimodal Large Language Models. While recognized as a promising paradigm for visual reasoning, the underlying mechanisms driving i...

item.title: Search More, Think Less: Rethinking Long-Horizon Agentic Search for Efficiency and Generalization
item.description: Recent deep research agents primarily improve performance by scaling reasoning depth, but this leads to high inference cost and latency in search-intensive scenarios. Moreover, generalization across heterogeneous research settings remain...

item.title: General Agent Evaluation
item.description: The promise of general-purpose agents - systems that perform tasks in unfamiliar environments without domain-specific engineering - remains largely unrealized. Existing agents are predominantly specialized, and while emerging implementat...

item.title: veScale-FSDP: Flexible and High-Performance FSDP at Scale
item.description: Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP), also known as ZeRO, is widely used for training large-scale models, featuring its flexibility and minimal intrusion on model code. However, current FSDP systems struggle with structure-aware training m...

item.title: Overconfident Errors Need Stronger Correction: Asymmetric Confidence Penalties for Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has become the leading paradigm for enhancing reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs). However, standard RLVR algorithms suffer from a well-documented pathology: while they improve...

item.title: What Makes a Good Query? Measuring the Impact of Human-Confusing Linguistic Features on LLM Performance
item.description: Large Language Model (LLM) hallucinations are usually treated as defects of the model or its decoding strategy. Drawing on classical linguistics, we argue that a query's form can also shape a listener's (and model's) response. We operati...

item.title: MedCLIPSeg: Probabilistic Vision-Language Adaptation for Data-Efficient and Generalizable Medical Image Segmentation
item.description: Medical image segmentation remains challenging due to limited annotations for training, ambiguous anatomical features, and domain shifts. While vision-language models such as CLIP offer strong cross-modal representations, their potential...

item.title: No One Size Fits All: QueryBandits for Hallucination Mitigation
item.description: Advanced reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs) have led to more frequent hallucinations; yet most mitigation work focuses on open-source models for post-hoc detection and parameter editing. The dearth of studies focusing...

item.title: Efficient Continual Learning in Language Models via Thalamically Routed Cortical Columns
item.description: Continual learning is a core requirement for deployed language models, yet standard training and fine-tuning pipelines remain brittle under non-stationary data. Online updates often induce catastrophic forgetting, while methods that impr...

item.title: The Trinity of Consistency as a Defining Principle for General World Models
item.description: The construction of World Models capable of learning, simulating, and reasoning about objective physical laws constitutes a foundational challenge in the pursuit of Artificial General Intelligence. Recent advancements represented by vide...

item.title: AgentDropoutV2: Optimizing Information Flow in Multi-Agent Systems via Test-Time Rectify-or-Reject Pruning
item.description: While Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) excel in complex reasoning, they suffer from the cascading impact of erroneous information generated by individual participants. Current solutions often resort to rigid structural engineering or expensive...

item.title: Accelerating Diffusion via Hybrid Data-Pipeline Parallelism Based on Conditional Guidance Scheduling
item.description: Diffusion models have achieved remarkable progress in high-fidelity image, video, and audio generation, yet inference remains computationally expensive. Nevertheless, current diffusion acceleration methods based on distributed parallelis...

item.title: EmbodMocap: In-the-Wild 4D Human-Scene Reconstruction for Embodied Agents
item.description: Human behaviors in the real world naturally encode rich, long-term contextual information that can be leveraged to train embodied agents for perception, understanding, and acting. However, existing capture systems typically rely on costl...

item.title: Causal Motion Diffusion Models for Autoregressive Motion Generation
item.description: Recent advances in motion diffusion models have substantially improved the realism of human motion synthesis. However, existing approaches either rely on full-sequence diffusion models with bidirectional generation, which limits temporal...

item.title: GeoWorld: Geometric World Models
item.description: Energy-based predictive world models provide a powerful approach for multi-step visual planning by reasoning over latent energy landscapes rather than generating pixels. However, existing approaches face two major challenges: (i) their l...

item.title: DyaDiT: A Multi-Modal Diffusion Transformer for Socially Favorable Dyadic Gesture Generation
item.description: Generating realistic conversational gestures are essential for achieving natural, socially engaging interactions with digital humans. However, existing methods typically map a single audio stream to a single speaker's motion, without con...

item.title: Retrieve and Segment: Are a Few Examples Enough to Bridge the Supervision Gap in Open-Vocabulary Segmentation?
item.description: Open-vocabulary segmentation (OVS) extends the zero-shot recognition capabilities of vision-language models (VLMs) to pixel-level prediction, enabling segmentation of arbitrary categories specified by text prompts. Despite recent progres...

item.title: VGG-T^3: Offline Feed-Forward 3D Reconstruction at Scale
item.description: We present a scalable 3D reconstruction model that addresses a critical limitation in offline feed-forward methods: their computational and memory requirements grow quadratically w.r.t. the number of input images. Our approach is built o...

item.title: DLT-Corpus: A Large-Scale Text Collection for the Distributed Ledger Technology Domain
item.description: We introduce DLT-Corpus, the largest domain-specific text collection for Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) research to date: 2.98 billion tokens from 22.12 million documents spanning scientific literature (37,440 publications), United...

item.title: MEG-to-MEG Transfer Learning and Cross-Task Speech/Silence Detection with Limited Data
item.description: Data-efficient neural decoding is a central challenge for speech brain-computer interfaces. We present the first demonstration of transfer learning and cross-task decoding for MEG-based speech models spanning perception and production. W...
